message:
fix(journal): reject descriptor/revoke/unknown blocks that reuse an already-committed sequence number

- Add committed_sequences.contains() guards at the top of the descriptor,
  revoke, and unknown-block-type match arms in replay_jbd2_inner, causing
  replay to stop scanning when it encounters a block whose sequence number
  has already been committed — this prevents a second descriptor or revoke
  with the same sequence from being treated as part of a new transaction
  after its commit block has already been processed
- Add replay_jbd2_descriptor_after_commit_same_sequence_is_discarded test
  verifying that a duplicate descriptor with seq=7 after commit(seq=7)
  does not replay its data block
- Add replay_jbd2_revoke_after_commit_same_sequence_is_discarded test
  verifying that a revoke block after commit does not retroactively
  revoke the already-committed write
- Add replay_jbd2_unknown_block_inside_transaction_discards_sequence test
  verifying that an unknown JBD2 block type mid-transaction causes the
  incomplete transaction to be discarded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
